name: MCP Servers (reference implementations)
description: The official reference MCP servers maintained by the Model Context Protocol project — filesystem, git, fetch, memory, and more. Educational starting points and SDK usage examples.
installCommand: See individual server READMEs under the repo — most run via `npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-<name>`
sourceNote: Repo checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. Some individual sub-server npm packages have since been deprecated in favor of vendor-hosted remote servers (see GitHub MCP Server entry) — verify per-server before relying on an npm install.

name: GitHub MCP Server
description: GitHub's official MCP server — read repositories and code, manage issues and PRs, analyze code, automate workflows. Available as a GitHub-hosted remote server or a local binary.
installCommand: Remote: connect to GitHub's hosted MCP endpoint. Local: download the binary from the repo's releases.
sourceNote: Repo checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. The old npm package (@modelcontextprotocol/server-github) is deprecated as of April 2025 — do not install that; use the current repo's remote/binary options.

name: Supabase MCP
description: Official MCP server connecting Supabase to AI assistants — manage tables, fetch config, query data.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Vercel MCP Handler
description: Vercel's official adapter for building MCP servers on Next.js, Nuxt, Svelte, and more. (Vercel also runs its own hosted MCP endpoint at mcp.vercel.com for project/deployment tools.)
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Figma MCP Server (Dev Mode)
description: Figma's official desktop MCP server — exposes selected-frame layout, structured React+Tailwind context, and design variables to AI coding agents.
installCommand: Enable in the Figma desktop app: Dev Mode toolbar → Inspect panel → "Enable desktop MCP server" (runs locally at http://127.0.0.1:3845/mcp)
sourceNote: Repo checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. No npm package — this is a built-in desktop-app feature, not an installable server.

name: Playwright MCP
description: Microsoft's official browser-automation MCP server — drives real browsers via Playwright's accessibility tree instead of screenshots. Works with Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code, and more.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Cloudflare MCP Server
description: Official Cloudflare MCP server — manage Cloudflare account resources (Workers, DNS, and more) with natural language.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Sentry MCP
description: Official Sentry MCP server for interacting with error tracking, issues, releases, and events via LLMs — optimized for coding assistants.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Stripe MCP
description: Official Stripe MCP server — customers, products, payments, invoices, refunds, and more via function calling. Also available as a hosted remote server.
sourceNote: Docs page (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Context7 MCP
description: Upstash's Context7 — up-to-date code documentation for LLMs and AI code editors. Resolves library names to current docs so agents stop citing stale APIs.
installCommand: npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp (or connect to the hosted endpoint at mcp.context7.com/mcp)
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Notion MCP Server
description: Official Notion MCP server for the Notion API — pages, databases, and data sources, exposed to AI assistants over stdio or Streamable HTTP.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07. Notion is prioritizing its remote hosted MCP going forward; this local server may eventually be sunset.

name: Atlassian MCP Server
description: Official remote MCP server for Atlassian — securely connects Jira, Confluence, Jira Service Management, Bitbucket, and Compass via OAuth 2.1 or API tokens.
installCommand: Connect your MCP client to Atlassian's hosted remote endpoint (OAuth 2.1 or API token) — see repo for setup
sourceNote: Repo checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. Remote-hosted; no npm install.

name: Neon MCP Server
description: Official Neon MCP server — manage Neon Postgres projects, branches, and run migrations in natural language via the Neon Management API.
sourceNote: Repo (HTTP 200) and npm package (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) both checked live 2026-07-07.

name: Sanity MCP
description: Official Sanity MCP — query and manage structured content via GROQ, now served as a hosted remote server.
sourceNote: Docs page checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. The older local npm package (@sanity/mcp-server) is deprecated/archived in favor of the hosted remote server at mcp.sanity.io — install command reflects the current path, not the deprecated package.

name: Slack MCP Server (reference)
description: The official reference MCP server for the Slack API — channels, messages, and workspace interaction for Claude and other MCP clients.
sourceNote: npm package confirmed live (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. Its documented subpath inside the reference-servers repo (src/slack) 404'd on direct check, likely due to repo restructuring — linking to the main repo instead of the stale subpath.

name: Linear MCP (official, remote)
description: Linear's official hosted MCP server — issues, projects, and teams for AI assistants, no local install required.
installCommand: Connect your MCP client to https://mcp.linear.app/sse
sourceNote: Docs page checked live (HTTP 200) 2026-07-07. Remote-only; the community npm packages found during research (e.g. jerhadf/linear-mcp-server) are explicitly deprecated in favor of this hosted endpoint, so they're excluded from this directory.

name: ForgeKit Figma MCP
description: Open-source MCP server syncing Figma variables/design tokens into typed theme configs for Chakra UI, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui.
sourceNote: npm package confirmed live (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) and authored by Rich Tillman 2026-07-07. Note: the package's own package.json declares a repo URL (github.com/effinrich/chakra-figma-mcp) that now 404s — linking instead to the live public repo of the same project name, confirmed reachable (HTTP 200).

name: ForgeKit Storybook MCP
description: Open-source (MIT) Storybook MCP — exposes Storybook metadata/argTypes to AI coding agents, companion to the ForgeKit Nx/UI/Storybook generator suite.
sourceNote: npm package confirmed live (registry.npmjs.org, HTTP 200) and authored by Rich Tillman 2026-07-07. Repo link confirmed reachable (HTTP 200) at its current org location.
